The model favors Australia at 44%, with Paraguay positioned as the underdog at 26%. The remaining probability mass—31%—lands on a draw, suggesting meaningful uncertainty about the outcome.

The win-loss gap of 18 percentage points indicates Australia enters as a clear statistical favorite, yet the draw probability of nearly one-third underscores that this is a competitive fixture rather than a blowout scenario. The spread reflects a match where all three outcomes carry real plausibility, though Australia's edge is substantial.

A striking divergence separates the model and market. The sportsbook consensus backs Paraguay at 44%—nearly 18 percentage points higher than the model—while pricing Australia at 27%, nearly 17 points lower. Polymarket aligns closely with the sportsbooks. The dashboard does not adjudicate between these sources; the gap is large enough to warrant attention from bettors seeking to understand which framework is more reliable for this pairing.
